title: At Fasnacht
setting: It's the first morning of Fasnacht. The streets are full of masks and Guggen bands. A costumed stranger pulls you in.

steps.speaker: Maskierte
steps.es: Hoi! Warum stahsch denn so am Rand? Chum is Gwüel!
steps.en: Hey! Why are you standing at the edge? Come into the crowd!

options.es: Ha, warum nöd! Ich bi no nie a de Fasnacht gsii.
options.en: Ha, why not! I've never been to Fasnacht before.
options.feedback: Game and honest — locals love showing a newcomer the ropes.
Fasnacht is for joining, not watching — let yourself be pulled in.
It is loud! But that's the whole point — embrace the chaos once.

steps.speaker: Maskierte
steps.es: Nie a de Fasnacht?! Denn wird's Ziit. Häsch e Larve?
steps.en: Never to Fasnacht?! Then it's high time. Do you have a mask?

options.es: Nei, no kei. Wo überchunnt mer eini?
options.en: No, not yet. Where do you get one?
options.feedback: Curious and willing — and now you're part of the tradition.
At Fasnacht, dressing up is half the fun — lean in rather than out.
The mask is the joy of it — for one day, being someone else is the gift.

steps.speaker: Maskierte
steps.es: Da, nimm mini Reserve-Larve. Jetz ghörsch dezue!
steps.en: Here, take my spare mask. Now you belong!

options.es: Merci viilmal! Jetz bin ich e richtige Fasnächtler.
options.en: Thank you so much! Now I'm a proper carnival-goer.
options.feedback: You accept the gesture with joy — that's the spirit exactly.

options.es: Bisch sicher? Das isch ja dini.
options.feedback: Considerate, and it'll be warmly waved off. A kind beat.
Reluctance deflates a generous moment. Take the joy on offer.

steps.speaker: Maskierte
steps.es: Chum, d'Guggemusig fangt aa! Tanz mit öis!
steps.en: Come, the Guggen band is starting! Dance with us!

options.es: Los gaht's! Ich tanze mit, au wenn ich's nöd cha!
options.en: Here we go! I'll dance along, even if I can't!
options.feedback: Pure Fasnacht — nobody cares how you dance, only that you do.
Willing and easy — you're fully in the swing of it now.
Today's the day to break that rule — the crowd hides everyone anyway.
